headline_signal.rec_title: Post a short opinion video reacting to the LACMA David Geffen Galleries opening — specifically the 'human scale vs. civic monument' tension — using Zumthor's reputation for intimacy as the hook
headline_signal.rec_reason: With no audience data available (empty clusters, themes, questions, requests, and style signals), this recommendation defaults to pure external momentum. LACMA's David Geffen Galleries is the highest-velocity architecture story of the week across ArchDaily, Dezeen, Designboom, and The Architect's Newspaper simultaneously, with a live critical debate that invites designer opinion. The 'Swiss master of intimate craft asked to build at LA civic scale' angle is inherently TikTok-friendly — it creates a clear narrative tension, requires no prior audience knowledge, and positions the creator as a commentator rather than a reporter. A 60–90 second 'hot take' or 'as a designer, here's what I actually see' format tends to perform on architectural TikTok because it signals expertise without requiring a building tour. Confidence is low because there is zero audience signal to validate any topic choice — this is a trend-opportunism pick only.
